summary: Audit Warehouse14 for latent bugs of the same class the owner just hit (admin-actionâ†’no-effect, missing volume, hidden 500, mobileâ†”server contract gaps)
logs: Auditing 5 latent-bug dimensions (grounded in code + live prod, read-only)â€¦
Confirmed 9 real findings after adversarial verification.

confirmed.title: Product detail "eBay" toggle writes legacy listed_on_ebay, which nothing in the eBay channel reads â€” toggling it never lists, enrolls, or changes anything on eBay
confirmed.evidence: Mobile PublishPanel: `void toggle.mutate({ listedOnEbay: !listedOnEbay })` (product/[id].tsx:1098) â†’ updateProduct PUT â†’ products.ts:364 `maybe('listedOnEbay', body.listedOnEbay, before.listedOnEbay)`. But the entire eBay channel is driven by `ebay_state`, NOT `listed_on_ebay`: products-ebay-publish.ts:184 sets `listedOnEbay: true` ONLY on a real marketplace publish, and the eBay screen filters/operates on ebay_state (ebay.tsx:139-140 'pipeline membership â€” NOT by the legacy `listedOnEbay` flag, which only flips on a marketplace publish'). api-client products.ts:104-107 documents `listedOnEbay` as 'Legacy operator-intent flag â€¦ flipped TRUE only by a real marketplace publish'.
confirmed.consumer: eBay channel (ebay_state pipeline / publish flow / ebay.tsx). The boolean the toggle writes is read by NO eBay behavior â€” only by the operator list-filter products-list.ts:77.
confirmed.whyBug: The owner flips the 'eBay' switch on a product expecting it to be listed on / enrolled in eBay. It writes a vestigial boolean: ebay_state stays NULL, no listing is created, the eBay screen still shows the item as not-enrolled, and the next real publish/transition is unaffected. The owner believes an item is on eBay when it is not. Symmetric to BUG-1 (UI writes field A; the effect depends on field B = ebay_state).
confirmed.proposedFix: Remove the free-standing `listedOnEbay` toggle from PublishPanel and route eBay listing through the eBay channel (ebayApi.transition to ENTWURF for enrollment / publishToEbay), as the dedicated eBay screen already does. If a read-only indicator is wanted here, show ebayState (not listedOnEbay) and deep-link to /ebay for the action. Do not let a PUT to listed_on_ebay masquerade as 'list on eBay'.
confirmed.verifyNote:
CONFIRMED, symmetric to BUG-1 (UI writes field A; the channel reads field B). Verified from code:

WRITER â€” apps/mobile/src/app/product/[id].tsx:1092-1098: the PublishPanel "eBay" Row (label "eBay", hint "Im eBay-Kanal gelistet") does `void toggle.mutate({ listedOnEbay: !listedOnEbay })`. The toggle hook (lines 975-989) sends a PUT updateProduct with only `listedOnEbay`. It never touches `ebay_state`.

PERSISTER â€” apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products.ts:364 `maybe('listedOnEbay', body.listedOnEbay, before.listedOnEbay)` writes products.listed_on_ebay and nothing else; ebay_state stays NULL.

CONSUMER â€” the entire eBay channel is driven by ebay_state, NOT listed_on_ebay:
- apps/mobile/src/app/ebay.tsx:138-143 fetches the pipeline with `enrolledOnEbay: true` (ebay_state IS NOT NULL) and explicitly comments it filters by real pipeline membership "NOT by the legacy `listedOnEbay` flag." So a PublishPanel-toggled item never appears on the pipeline board.
- ebay.tsx:838-846 the enroll search uses `enrolledOnEbay: false` (ebay_state IS NULL), with a comment that the legacy listedOnEbay flag would NOT exclude an already-"listed" item. So the toggled item STILL shows up in the eBay screen as not-yet-enrolled â€” directly contradicting the toggle.
- The real marketplace push (apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products-ebay-publish.ts:175-186) is the ONLY place that sets listedOnEbay=TRUE, and it does so together with ebay_state='ONLINE' on a genuine publish.
- packages/api-client/src/domains/products.ts:102-113 documents listedOnEbay as a "Legacy operator-intent flag â€¦ flipped TRUE only by a real marketplace publish" and points callers to enrolledOnEbay.
- apps/mobile/dev/ebay-pipeline-proof.mjs is a committed proof that filtering the pipeline by listedOnEbay was a confirmed P0/P1 root cause, already migrated to ebay_state everywhere â€” except this PublishPanel toggle and the dashboard badge.

OPERATOR-FACING HARM â€” apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:714-718: when listedOnEbay is TRUE the dashboard product row renders an "Online" (Globe) badge, so the false belief is actively reinforced. The owner flips "eBay", sees "Online", believes the item is on eBay â€” yet ebay_state is NULL, no listing/offer exists, and the dedicated /ebay screen still treats it as un-enrolled.

Adversarial checks that did NOT void it: the flag is consumed elsewhere only by operator list-filters (products-list.ts:77) and display badges â€” NOT by any eBay listing/enrollment behavior, so the write is genuinely effect-free for its stated purpose. The throw/validation paths are irrelevant. Not by-design: schema docs, ebay.tsx comments, and the proof script all class reliance on this flag as the known-wrong legacy pattern.

Severity P1 (not P0): real misleading-write defect with operator consequence (false "Online" badge + belief item is enrolled), but it corrupts no data, does not break the working /ebay enroll path (which ignores this flag), and the actual live eBay push is an honest not-configured stub pre-go-live (EBAY_OAUTH_TOKEN empty â†’ no HTTP), so the practical blast radius is a misleading indicator rather than a silent public-facing failure or lost sale.

confirmed.title: "Im Laden" toggle (listed_on_storefront) silently controls ONLY whether the WhatsApp AI assistant can find the product â€” its labelled meaning (visible on the shop counter / catalog) is wrong, and online-published items are invisible to the bot
confirmed.where: apps/mobile/src/app/product/[id].tsx:1070 (writer) â†’ products.ts:363 persists products.listedOnStorefront; consumer apps/api-cloud/src/lib/whatsapp-bot-tools.ts:65
confirmed.evidence: Writer: `void toggle.mutate({ listedOnStorefront: !listedOnStorefront })` with hint 'Auf der Ladentheke sichtbar' (product/[id].tsx:1065-1070). The public catalog and cart deliberately migrated OFF this flag to `is_published_to_web`: storefront-catalog.ts:394 `WHERE p.is_published_to_web = TRUE`, and storefront-cart.ts:192-194 comment: 'the cart used to gate on the separate `listed_on_storefront`, which the publish flow never set â†’ every "published" product was silently un-buyable.' The ONLY remaining behavioral consumer of listed_on_storefront is the WhatsApp AI bot: whatsapp-bot-tools.ts:64-65 `WHERE status = 'AVAILABLE' AND listed_on_storefront = TRUE`.
confirmed.consumer: WhatsApp AI assistant searchInventory (whatsapp-bot-tools.ts:65). The customer-facing storefront catalog/cart do NOT read listed_on_storefront.
confirmed.whyBug: Two-way mismatch. (a) Toggling 'Im Laden' on/off does nothing to the shop counter or the web catalog it implies; it only flips whether the WhatsApp bot recommends the item. (b) An item the owner publishes online via 'Im Online-Shop' (isPublishedToWeb=TRUE) but leaves 'Im Laden' OFF is fully live and buyable on the web yet the WhatsApp assistant will never surface it â€” a published product the bot insists is unavailable.
confirmed.proposedFix: Decide the bot's true gate and align it. Most likely the WhatsApp searchInventory should gate on `is_published_to_web = TRUE` (same as the catalog/cart), making it consistent with what customers actually see online. Then either repurpose or remove the 'Im Laden' toggle so the UI label matches its real effect.
confirmed.verifyNote: Confirmed from the actual code. Writer: apps/mobile/src/app/product/[id].tsx:1064-1070 â€” the "Im Laden" toggle, labelled "Auf der Ladentheke sichtbar", calls toggle.mutate({ listedOnStorefront: !listedOnStorefront }). Persisted at apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products.ts:363. The customer-facing storefront deliberately migrated OFF this flag: storefront-catalog.ts:394 filters WHERE p.is_published_to_web = TRUE AND p.status='AVAILABLE' (no listed_on_storefront), and storefront-cart.ts:191-194 carries an explicit comment that the cart used to gate on listed_on_storefront â€” which the publish flow never set, making every "published" product un-buyable â€” and now gates on is_published_to_web. Migration 0029 (packages/db/migrations/0029_storefront_publishing.sql) explicitly marks listed_on_storefront as "kept; deprecated" and says the storefront trusts is_published_to_web. Meanwhile the live WhatsApp AI assistant still gates on the deprecated flag: whatsapp-bot-tools.ts:64-65 searchInventory WHERE status='AVAILABLE' AND listed_on_storefront = TRUE, and this is wired into the running bot (whatsapp-bot-runner.ts:116 / social-bot-runner.ts:108). The two-way mismatch is demonstrable: (a) toggling "Im Laden" changes nothing on the web catalog/cart its label promises, only whether the bot can find the item; (b) a product published with isPublishedToWeb=TRUE but listed_on_storefront=FALSE is fully buyable on the web yet invisible to the WhatsApp assistant. This is the same class as BUG-1 (consumer reads a different field than the labelled UI writes) plus the BUG-1 root cause (the publish flow never sets listed_on_storefront). One correction to the finding's "ONLY remaining consumer" wording: listed_on_storefront is also read by the admin products-list optional filter (products-list.ts:74) and the mobile inventory list channel chip (index.tsx:706, also labelled "Im Laden"); however neither is a customer-facing storefront surface, so the core defect â€” the toggle's labelled meaning is wrong and online-published items are invisible to the bot â€” stands. P1 is appropriate: it silently misleads the operator and suppresses sellable inventory from the only AI sales channel, but does not corrupt data or break checkout.

confirmed.title: Dashboard product tiles show channel badges from listed_on_storefront / listed_on_ebay, so genuinely web-published items show no 'Online' badge and the 'Online' badge actually tracks the legacy eBay-publish flag
confirmed.evidence: index.tsx:706 `{item.listedOnStorefront || item.listedOnEbay ? (` then renders 'Im Laden' from listedOnStorefront (708-712) and 'Online' from listedOnEbay (714-718). Web publication state is `is_published_to_web` (storefront-catalog.ts:394), which is NOT consulted here; and listedOnEbay only flips on a real eBay marketplace publish (products.ts api-client:104-107, products-ebay-publish.ts:184).
confirmed.consumer: Schatzkammer dashboard channel indicator (cosmetic, but owner-facing truth signal).
confirmed.whyBug: The dashboard's at-a-glance 'where is this item selling' badges are wrong: an item published to the web shows no 'Online' tag, and the 'Online' tag is driven by the legacy eBay flag rather than either real channel. The owner reads false channel state on the home screen.
confirmed.proposedFix: Derive the badges from the real channel signals: 'Online' (web) from isPublishedToWeb, and an 'eBay' badge from ebayState != null (or ebayState=='ONLINE'). Surface isPublishedToWeb in the product list row (it is already on ProductDetail; add it to ProductListRow / products-list.ts projection) so the dashboard can read it.
confirmed.verifyNote: CONFIRMED, same class as BUG-1 (UI surface reads a different field than the one that governs the channel). At apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:706-719 the dashboard ProductRow renders sale-channel badges purely from `item.listedOnStorefront` (â†’ "Im Laden", Store icon) and `item.listedOnEbay` (â†’ "Online", Globe icon). The real web-publish gate is a distinct column: packages/db schema (products.js:140-149) documents `is_published_to_web` as the SINGLE source of truth for "show on warehouse14.de", and storefront-catalog.ts:394 filters exactly on `is_published_to_web = TRUE AND status='AVAILABLE'`. `listed_on_ebay` is a legacy operator-intent flag that, per products-ebay-publish.ts:184, only flips TRUE on a real eBay marketplace push (api-client products.ts:101-112 documents this explicitly). So the Globe/"Online" badge tracks the eBay flag, not web publication, and `is_published_to_web` is never consulted on this surface. Crucially, `isPublishedToWeb` is NOT even present on ProductListRow (api-client products.ts:70-87; products-list.ts:153-154,255-256 project only listedOnStorefront/listedOnEbay), whereas it IS on ProductDetail (products.ts:219) â€” so the dashboard physically cannot read the correct signal without a projection change. Live prod confirms the divergence is real and total: of products, 14 have is_published_to_web=TRUE and ALL 14 have listed_on_ebay=FALSE (â†’ 14 web-published items show NO "Online" badge), while all 5 listed_on_ebay=TRUE rows are NOT web-published (â†’ the "Online" badge fires only on items that are NOT on the web). Zero correlation between the badge and actual web state. Not by-design: the badge is labeled "Online" with a web/Globe glyph, misrepresenting the eBay-intent flag as web-channel truth. Severity P2 is appropriate: this is a cosmetic at-a-glance owner-facing truth signal on the home screen, not a functional gate â€” unlike BUG-1 nothing is mis-routed or fails to publish; the owner just reads false channel state. Proposed fix (surface isPublishedToWeb on the list projection; drive "Online" from it and an "eBay" badge from ebayState/listedOnEbay) is sound.

confirmed.title: The /data/photos & /data/kyc writability fix is live-only â€” it is absent from the Dockerfiles and docker-compose.prod.yml, so any volume/host recreation reintroduces the BUG-2 EACCES for both product photos and KYC images
confirmed.where: infrastructure/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml:88-90,116-118 (volume mounts) + apps/api-cloud/Dockerfile:30-43 and apps/worker/Dockerfile:22-34 (runtime stage, USER node, no chown/mkdir of /data)
confirmed.evidence: Live `ls -ld` in the api container shows the fix was applied ONLY at runtime, NOT in any committed file: `drwxr-xr-x 2 node node /data/kyc` (a manual chown) and `drwxrwxrwx 55 root root /data/photos` (a manual chmod 777). Neither Dockerfile creates or chowns /data/photos or /data/kyc (they only `chmod -R go+rX /app`), and compose just declares `- photosdata:/data/photos` / `- kycdata:/data/kyc` with no init/entrypoint/chown. Docker creates a never-in-image volume mountpoint as root:root 0755 â€” exactly the BUG-2 root cause. The container runs `USER node` (uid=1000(node), confirmed live), and photo-store.ts:108 `mkdir(dirFor(env,id))` + kyc-store.ts:162 `mkdir(join(env.KYC_PHOTOS_DIR,...))` then write shard subdirs under those roots.
confirmed.consumer: apps/api-cloud/src/lib/photo-store.ts:108-111 (product-photo WebP writes) and apps/api-cloud/src/lib/kyc-store.ts:162-163 (encrypted KYC .enc writes) â€” both mkdir+writeFile as uid 1000 under the volume root
confirmed.whyBug: BUG-2 is only papered over on the current running server, not actually fixed in the source of truth. The moment the owner recreates the photosdata/kycdata volume, redeploys onto a fresh host, or restores from backup, Docker recreates the mountpoint as root:root and uid-1000 node hits 500 EACCES again: product-photo upload fails AND legally-required KYC/Ausweis image capture (GwG/DSGVO) fails â€” silently, because nothing in the repo encodes the fix. It is undocumented tribal knowledge that will not survive disaster recovery.
confirmed.proposedFix: Encode the fix in committed infra so it self-heals: in both apps/api-cloud/Dockerfile and apps/worker/Dockerfile runtime stage, before `USER node` add `RUN mkdir -p /data/photos /data/kyc && chown -R 1000:1000 /data` (creates the in-image dirs so Docker propagates node ownership to the named volume on first mount). Prefer 0700 on /data/kyc, not the live 0777 currently on /data/photos. Alternatively add an init/entrypoint that chowns the mounts at startup. Then rebuild & redeploy so the durable image matches the live hand-patched state.
confirmed.verifyNote: CONFIRMED from source + live read-only checks. (1) apps/api-cloud/Dockerfile:30-43 and apps/worker/Dockerfile:22-34 both set USER node and run ONLY `chmod -R go+rX /app`; neither mkdir's or chown's /data, /data/photos, or /data/kyc. (2) docker-compose.prod.yml:88-90,116-118 declares named volumes photosdata:/data/photos and kycdata:/data/kyc with NO `user:`, NO entrypoint/init, NO chown. (3) Live `docker exec warehouse14-api` shows id=uid=1000(node); /data is `root:root 0755`; /data/kyc is `node node` (a manual chown) and /data/photos is `root:root 0777` (a manual chmod 777) â€” ownership/mode that cannot come from the image (never touches /data) or compose, so it was hand-applied on the host. (4) The deployed image entrypoint is the stock node `docker-entrypoint.sh` with User=node, which does not chown /data. (5) Consumers confirmed: photo-store.ts:107-112 `mkdir(dirFor,...)` + writeFile and kyc-store.ts:161-163 `mkdir(join(KYC_PHOTOS_DIR, shard))` + writeFile both run as uid 1000 and the first write must create a shard dir directly under the volume root â€” on a fresh `root:root 0755` mountpoint that mkdir gets EACCES, exactly the BUG-2 root cause. (6) docs/runbooks/0078-kyc-volume.md:31 itself documents the fix as a manual live `docker compose exec -u root api chown -R node:node /data/kyc`; the committed compose change added the kyc volume MOUNT but the ownership fix that makes either volume writable by uid 1000 lives nowhere in the repo. Therefore any volume recreation, fresh-host redeploy, or backup/DR restore recreates the mountpoint as root:root 0755 and silently reintroduces 500 EACCES for product-photo upload AND legally-retained KYC/Ausweis image capture. Same class as the seed bugs (write path lacking durable, encoded permission/persistence). Severity P1 (latent, not currently broken on the running host, but breaks legally-required KYC capture on any recreate with no in-repo self-heal). Not a false positive: the field is consumed, but the path is NOT durably writable by source; the live writability is an undocumented-in-image hand patch, exactly as claimed. Proposed fix (add `RUN mkdir -p /data/photos /data/kyc && chown -R 1000:1000 /data` before USER node in both runtime stages, or an entrypoint chown) is sound; note 0700 on /data/kyc is preferable to the live 0777.

confirmed.title: R2 presign routes (/api/photos/upload-url and POST /api/products/:id/photos) hard-500 in prod because R2_BUCKET is empty â€” same class as the KYC-volume bug
confirmed.evidence: r2.ts:25 `if (!env.R2_ACCOUNT_ID || !env.R2_BUCKET || ...) throw new Error('R2 not configured ...')`. Prod `.env` has `R2_BUCKET=` (empty) â€” confirmed live via `ssh myserver` grep. Both routes catch the throw and re-raise it as `R2NotConfiguredError` with `httpStatus = 500`. photo-direct-upload.ts's own header comment states the reality: 'R2 is unset in production (R2_BUCKET empty)'. tauri-pos calls this on its happy path: apps/tauri-pos/src/lib/photo-upload.ts:87 and apps/tauri-pos/src/screens/secondary/Dokumente.tsx:386 both do `photosApi.requestUploadUrl(...)` â†’ POST /api/photos/upload-url.
confirmed.consumer: tauri-pos document/photo capture (`requestUploadUrl` in photo-upload.ts + Dokumente.tsx) â€” every upload attempt gets a 500. The local-disk replacement POST /api/photos/upload exists and works, but these two R2 routes were never migrated to it.
confirmed.whyBug: An owner/cashier on tauri-pos who scans a document or photographs a product hits an unconditional 500 on normal input â€” there is no R2 in prod, so the route can never succeed. It is the BUG-2 pattern exactly: a write path whose external dependency is unconfigured in prod while a sibling path (local store) was migrated. The mobile Owner OS is unaffected because it already uses /api/photos/upload.
confirmed.proposedFix: Either (a) repoint tauri-pos's requestUploadUrl/photo-upload.ts + Dokumente.tsx at the working local-disk route POST /api/photos/upload (the same migration the mobile app already received), or (b) have photo-upload-url.ts and products.ts /photos detect the unconfigured-R2 condition and fall back to / return a clear 503/'use local upload' contract instead of a 500. Long-term, delete the dead R2 presign routes if no client should use R2 in prod.
confirmed.verifyNote:
CONFIRMED real, same class as BUG-2. Verified each link of the chain against code + live prod:

1) r2.ts:25 `getR2Client` throws 'R2 not configuredâ€¦' if `!env.R2_BUCKET` (among others). env.ts gives R2_BUCKET a default of '' (empty), so an empty value passes schema validation and reaches the throw.

2) Live prod confirms the unconfigured condition: `ssh myserver` sudo-grep of /opt/warehouse14/.env shows `R2_BUCKET=` (empty) while R2_ACCOUNT_ID/ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET_ACCESS_KEY are populated. docker-compose.prod.yml line 78 comment: 'Local product-photo store (replaces the empty R2 bucket).' photo-direct-upload.ts header: 'R2 is unset in production (R2_BUCKET empty).'

3) Both presign routes are registered (app.ts:239-240) and both catch getPresignedPutUrl's throw and re-raise as R2NotConfiguredError with httpStatus=500 (photo-upload-url.ts:101-103, products.ts:830-832). So on normal input they ALWAYS 500 in prod â€” unreachable-success, not edge case.

4) Live consumer confirmed: Dokumente.tsx:386 calls photosApi.requestUploadUrl directly inside its upload mutation's happy path (setStage signingâ†’puttingâ†’registering), with no R2-unconfigured fallback. Dokumente is a registered, reachable secondary surface (surface-registry.ts:282-289). An operator uploading any Ausweis/Rechnung/Expertise document gets an unconditional 500. The sibling durable route POST /api/photos/upload (local disk) exists and works (photo-direct-upload.ts) but Dokumente was never migrated to it.

CORRECTION to the finding (does not invalidate it, but narrows blast radius): the product-photo path is NOT affected. tauri-pos Fotos.tsx:173 uses uploadProductPhotoViaApi â†’ photosApi.uploadDirect (the local-disk /api/photos/upload), not the R2 presign route. The finding's cited lib/photo-upload.ts:87 lives in uploadBlobToR2, which is DEAD CODE â€” grep shows zero callers in tauri-pos. Likewise products.ts:824 /photos R2 presign route has no live tauri-pos consumer. So the only genuinely-broken live surface is Dokumente (document capture), not product photography.

Net: a real, reachable, deterministic prod 500 on a normal operator action (document upload), caused by a write path whose external dependency is unconfigured in prod while its sibling was migrated â€” textbook BUG-2 pattern. P1 is fair (it's one secondary surface, not the product happy path the finding overstates, but it's fully broken with no fallback). Fix: repoint Dokumente at POST /api/photos/upload (same migration the mobile/Fotos paths already got), or have the presign routes return a clear 'use local upload' contract; and delete the dead uploadBlobToR2 + unused R2 presign routes.

confirmed.title: Dashboard inventory list shows the legacy storefront/eBay flags as the online-shop status, never the real `isPublishedToWeb` gate (same class as BUG-1)
confirmed.evidence: index.tsx:706 `{item.listedOnStorefront || item.listedOnEbay ? (` then :708 `listedOnStorefront ? <Store/> 'Im Laden'` and :714 `item.listedOnEbay ? <Globe size={10}/> <Text>Online</Text>`. The api-client `ProductListRow` (products.ts:58 `listedOnStorefront: boolean;` / `listedOnEbay: boolean;`) has NO `isPublishedToWeb` field, and the list route mapping (products-list.ts:254-256) returns only `listedOnStorefront`/`listedOnEbay`/`isCommission` â€” `isPublishedToWeb` is never selected or returned. The storefront catalog filters on `is_published_to_web=TRUE AND status='AVAILABLE'` (per the BUG-1 note).
confirmed.consumer: index.tsx sale-channel indicator row â€” the `Globe`+"Online" badge bound to `item.listedOnEbay`
confirmed.whyBug: The owner manages a product's true online-shop visibility from the detail PublishPanel via `isPublishedToWeb` (the field the storefront actually reads â€” this was the fix for BUG-1). But the dashboard Lager list, the surface the owner scans every day, derives its "Online" badge from the legacy `listedOnEbay` flag and a globe icon, and its "Im Laden" badge from `listedOnStorefront`. So a product correctly published to warehouse14.de (isPublishedToWeb=TRUE) shows NO online indicator unless it also has the unrelated eBay flag set, and a product that merely has the legacy storefront flag shows "Im Laden" while being invisible online. The list reflects fields that do not govern publication â€” the owner cannot trust the dashboard to tell him what is actually live on the web shop.
confirmed.proposedFix: Add `isPublishedToWeb: boolean` to ProductListRow (products.ts) and select+return `products.isPublishedToWeb` in products-list.ts:254-256 mapping. Then in index.tsx drive the "Online-Shop" indicator off `item.isPublishedToWeb` (gated, like the detail hint, on status==='AVAILABLE' for the 'live now' vs 'pending' nuance), and relabel the `listedOnEbay` badge as "eBay" (with the Tag glyph used in PublishPanel) rather than a globe+"Online".
confirmed.verifyNote: Confirmed real, same class as BUG-1 (consumer reads a different field than the one that governs the behaviour). Verified end-to-end: (1) Consumer apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:706-721 derives "Im Laden" from item.listedOnStorefront and a Globe+"Online" badge from item.listedOnEbay, and never references isPublishedToWeb (grep: NONE in index.tsx). (2) The list-row type ProductListRow in packages/api-client/src/domains/products.ts:54-87 carries listedOnStorefront/listedOnEbay but has NO isPublishedToWeb field. (3) The list route apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products-list.ts:255-256 maps only listedOnStorefront/listedOnEbay/isCommission; isPublishedToWeb is never selected or returned (grep: NONE in products-list.ts). (4) Ground truth: the storefront catalog filters on p.is_published_to_web = TRUE AND status='AVAILABLE' (storefront-catalog.ts:394,521). (5) The three columns are independent in the DB schema (packages/db/src/schema/products/products.ts:108,109,192 â€” separate defaults + a separate listed_on_ebay index) and the detail PublishPanel toggles each one independently (product/[id].tsx:1070 listedOnStorefront, :1087 isPublishedToWeb, :1098 listedOnEbay) with no server-side sync, so listedOnStorefront/listedOnEbay genuinely do NOT track web publication. (6) The detail path proves the gap: products-detail.ts:250 returns isPublishedToWeb and product/[id].tsx:627 feeds it to PublishPanel, but the list path omits it. Net effect: a product correctly published to warehouse14.de (isPublishedToWeb=TRUE, eBay off) shows no online indicator in the dashboard list, while a product with only the legacy listedOnEbay flag shows a misleading globe+"Online" though it is not on the web shop â€” the daily-scan surface misrepresents true web-shop visibility. Not a false positive: isPublishedToWeb is the field the storefront actually reads and it is demonstrably absent from this surface. Severity is borderline P1/P2 â€” it is a display/trust defect on a secondary surface (the authoritative control and correct state live on the detail PublishPanel, no data loss or broken write), but it actively misleads the owner about live web state on the surface he scans most, so I keep it at P1.

confirmed.title: api-client `ProductListRow` under-declares fields the list route actually returns (silent loss of stamp/collector data at the type boundary)
confirmed.evidence: The server list mapping returns `stampErhaltung` (products-list.ts:243), `stampMinr` (:249), `yearMintedFrom` (:250), `yearMintedTo` (:251), `originCountry` (:252), `period` (:253), `catalogReference` (:254) on every list row, but the api-client `ProductListRow` interface declares none of them â€” it stops at `barcode`, `primaryPhotoThumbUrl`, channel flags and Lagerort triplet.
confirmed.consumer: any mobile list-row consumer that would want stamp/collector facts (e.g. a Briefmarken Lager row) â€” the data arrives over the wire but is typed away
confirmed.whyBug: This is the inverse of BUG-1 (server returns MORE than the client types), so it is not a runtime no-op, but it silently strips the new Briefmarken (`stampErhaltung`/`stampMinr`) and collector metadata from the typed surface: the mobile list can never render Michel number or Erhaltung on a row even though the payload already carries them, and a developer reading the type assumes the server does not send them. It also drifts the api-client out of sync with the route it documents itself as mirroring.
confirmed.proposedFix: Add the missing optional/nullable fields (`stampErhaltung`, `stampMinr`, `yearMintedFrom`, `yearMintedTo`, `originCountry`, `period`, `catalogReference`) to `ProductListRow` to match the products-list.ts response, or remove them from the route response if list rows are not meant to carry them. Add a contract test asserting the two field sets are equal.
confirmed.verifyNote:
CONFIRMED as a genuine type-contract drift, correctly rated P2.

Server side: apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products-list.ts selects all 7 stamp/collector fields in the Drizzle query (originCountry/period/catalogReference at lines 150-152, plus stampErhaltung/stampMinr/yearMintedFrom/yearMintedTo above) and emits every one of them on each list row in the `.send()` mapping (lines 243-254: stampErhaltung, stampMinr, yearMintedFrom, yearMintedTo, originCountry, period, catalogReference). So the data really is on the wire for every list row.

Client side: packages/api-client/src/domains/products.ts `ProductListRow` (lines 54-87) declares none of these 7 fields â€” it ends at barcode, primaryPhotoThumbUrl, status/condition/itemType/metal/weight/price/name/desc, channel flags, isCommission, the Lagerort triplet, archivedAt, createdAt. A targeted grep for stampErhaltung/stampMinr/catalogReference/originCountry inside api-client returns hits only in ProductDetailRow (lines 205-285) and the create/update payload types (367-368) â€” never in ProductListRow. So the list type genuinely under-declares relative to its own detail type and the route it mirrors.

The type is consumed broadly (apps/mobile index.tsx, ebay.tsx, drucken.tsx, suche.tsx, search-ui.ts, scan-resolve.ts), so the missing fields are invisible to every typed list consumer.

Adversarial checks against false-positive criteria: (1) Not by-design â€” ProductDetailRow declares these same fields, so the omission on ProductListRow is an oversight from the Briefmarken feature add, not intentional asymmetry. (2) Not a runtime no-op like BUG-1 and not data loss â€” TS interfaces don't strip runtime JSON props; the bytes arrive and could be read via a cast. (3) The route header comments document the api-client as mirroring this response, so the drift is real contract divergence.

Correctly characterized and correctly severity-rated: this is a type-safety / DX defect (the mobile list cannot type-safely render Michel number or Erhaltung even though the payload carries them), not a functional outage. P2 is appropriate â€” no end-user breakage, no silent admin no-op, purely a typed-surface and contract-sync gap. Proposed fix (add the optional/nullable fields to ProductListRow + a contract test) is sound.

confirmed.title: SOLD-but-still-published products return a hard 404 on their public product page (no "verkauft" state) â€” bad for SEO + inbound links
confirmed.evidence: Live DB: 11 products are status='SOLD' AND is_published_to_web=TRUE. PDP probe: curl /api/storefront/products/hh â†’ 404, /api/storefront/products/roman-a73312 â†’ 404, while /api/storefront/products/schluessel (AVAILABLE) â†’ 200. The detail SQL filters `WHERE p.slug = ${slug} AND p.is_published_to_web = TRUE AND p.status = 'AVAILABLE'`. The storefront response schema (apps/api-cloud/src/schemas/storefront-catalog.ts) carries NO status field, so the page cannot even render a 'sold' state.
confirmed.consumer: warehouse14.de product detail page (Next.js storefront) / Google + any inbound link to a previously-published item
confirmed.whyBug: The owner's 'publish online' action wrote is_published_to_web=TRUE (the catalog-list field â€” exactly the field BUG-1 was about). The item was listed, indexed, linked. When it sells, the LIST correctly drops it, but the DETAIL page that customers/Google already have bookmarked returns a hard 404 instead of a 'verkauft' page or a 410 Gone. Same field-vs-consumer mismatch shape as BUG-1: one surface (list) honors the publish state one way, the other surface (detail) silently breaks. 11 live URLs are dead right now.
confirmed.proposedFix: In the :slug detail handler, drop `AND p.status='AVAILABLE'` from the gate (keep is_published_to_web=TRUE). Add a `status`/`isAvailable` field to StorefrontProduct schema + toStorefrontProduct projection so the PDP can render a 'verkauft / nicht mehr verfĂĽgbar' state with 200 (preserves SEO, lets the storefront show sold-archive). Alternatively return 410 Gone for SOLD so crawlers de-index cleanly. Do NOT keep the silent 404.
confirmed.verifyNote: CONFIRMED against code, live API, and live DB. apps/api-cloud/src/routes/storefront-catalog.ts:520-522 â€” the GET /api/storefront/products/:slug detail handler gates on `WHERE p.slug = ${slug} AND p.is_published_to_web = TRUE AND p.status = 'AVAILABLE'` and throws StorefrontProductNotFoundError (404) when no row matches (lines 526-527). The response schema StorefrontProduct (apps/api-cloud/src/schemas/storefront-catalog.ts) carries no status/isAvailable field and toStorefrontProduct (line 206) projects none, so a 'verkauft' state cannot be rendered even in principle. Live probes: /api/storefront/products/schluessel (AVAILABLE) -> 200; /hh and /roman-a73312 -> 404 â€” exactly as claimed. Live DB confirms hh=SOLD/published, roman-a73312=SOLD/published, schluessel=AVAILABLE/published, and count(status='SOLD' AND is_published_to_web=TRUE)=11. So 11 previously-published, indexable URLs return a hard 404. This is the same field-vs-consumer mismatch shape as BUG-1: the owner's publish action sets is_published_to_web=TRUE (item indexed/linked); on sale the detail page silently 404s with no 410 Gone and no sold-archive page. Note: the list endpoint dropping SOLD items is explicitly by-design (line 298-301 comment), and that part is correct â€” but that does NOT rescue the detail page; a previously-indexed PDP returning a hard 404 (vs 410 or a 200 'verkauft' page) is a genuine SEO/inbound-link defect, not by-design. Severity P2 rather than P1: it is real with live impact, but it degrades SEO/UX gracefully (correct-ish 'not found'), not a broken core action, data corruption, security, or money path; the consuming Next.js storefront is a separate deployment not in this repo, so the exact rendered page could not be inspected here, but the 404 at the API boundary is definitive.

confirmed.title: Category facet filter (descendant CTE) does not exclude hidden_from_storefront categories â€” a product filed only under a hidden category can be surfaced by ?category=<hidden-slug>
confirmed.where: apps/api-cloud/src/routes/storefront-catalog.ts:400-420 (the EXISTS + WITH RECURSIVE cat_descendants block in GET /api/storefront/products)
confirmed.evidence: The list endpoint's primaryCategory LEFT JOIN correctly gates `c.hidden_from_storefront = FALSE` (line 393), but the OPTIONAL category facet uses a separate path: `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_categories pc2 ... WHERE pc2.category_id IN (WITH RECURSIVE cat_descendants AS (SELECT id FROM categories WHERE slug = ${category}) ...))`. Neither the seed slug lookup nor the recursive descend filters `hidden_from_storefront`. Live DB has a hidden category: slug='ankauf', hidden_from_storefront=t. curl ?category=ankauf currently returns [] ONLY because no published+AVAILABLE product is filed under it today â€” the guard is absent, not satisfied.
confirmed.consumer: warehouse14.de category-filter URLs (?category=ankauf or any future hidden category)
confirmed.whyBug: hidden_from_storefront is the owner's 'do not show this taxonomy publicly' switch. The /categories tree honors it, and the product-card's primaryCategory honors it, but the FILTER path ignores it â€” so an internal-only bucket (Ankauf) is still a working public query string the moment any published item lands in it. Latent today, but it is the same write-flag-honored-in-one-place-not-another class. An over-permissive facet is the inverse of an over-strict WHERE.
confirmed.proposedFix: Add `AND hidden_from_storefront = FALSE` to the base case of cat_descendants (the `SELECT id FROM categories WHERE slug = ...`) and to the recursive `JOIN categories child`. That way a hidden category slug resolves to an empty descendant set and the facet matches nothing public.
confirmed.verifyNote:
CONFIRMED in apps/api-cloud/src/routes/storefront-catalog.ts (GET /api/storefront/products). The list endpoint gates the primaryCategory display via the LEFT JOIN at line 393 (`ON c.id = pc.category_id AND c.hidden_from_storefront = FALSE`), and the /categories tree at line 573 filters `WHERE hidden_from_storefront = FALSE`. But the OPTIONAL category facet (lines 400-420) is a fully separate path: an EXISTS over product_categories pc2 whose membership set comes from `WITH RECURSIVE cat_descendants` â€” base case `SELECT id FROM categories WHERE slug = ${category}` (line 411) and recursive step `JOIN categories child` (lines 413-415). Neither carries `hidden_from_storefront`. That EXISTS is independent of the LEFT-JOINed `c` (a hidden primary category only nulls the display fields via LEFT JOIN, it does not exclude the row), so the only mandatory predicates remain is_published_to_web=TRUE AND status='AVAILABLE'. Therefore a published+AVAILABLE product filed under a hidden category (e.g. 'ankauf') WOULD be surfaced by ?category=<hidden-slug>.

Live read-only verification (https://api.warehouse14.de): the /api/storefront/categories tree does NOT contain 'ankauf' (hidden honored there), yet GET /api/storefront/products?category=ankauf returns total=0/items=0 â€” confirming the facet accepts the hidden slug as a valid query and returns empty only because no qualifying product is filed under it today, not because any guard rejects hidden categories. Guard is absent, not satisfied â€” exactly as the finding states.

This is the same class as the seed bugs (a flag honored on one sibling query path but ignored on another). It is latent (currently zero matching rows) and the hidden-category leak is taxonomy exposure rather than PII/price leakage, so P2 is appropriate. Proposed fix (add `AND hidden_from_storefront = FALSE` to the CTE base case and recursive JOIN) is correct and sufficient.

result.report:
All findings are confirmed and verified. My job is synthesis, not re-verification. Let me dedupe, merge same-root-cause items, rank by owner impact, and produce the report.

Let me analyze the merge opportunities:

- Findings #3 (dashboard tiles badges) and #6 (dashboard inventory list `isPublishedToWeb`) are the **same root cause**: `apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:703-721` renders channel badges from legacy `listedOnStorefront`/`listedOnEbay` because `ProductListRow` / `products-list.ts` never project `isPublishedToWeb`. Merge them.
- Everything else is distinct.

Here is the synthesized report.

---

# Latent-bug hunt â€” synthesis report (warehouse14)

Two owner-found bugs (BUG-1: UI writes field A, consumer reads field B; BUG-2: write path with no durable permission/persistence in prod) seeded this hunt. Every finding below is the **same class** and adversarially confirmed against code + live prod. 9 raw findings â†’ **8 after merge** (two dashboard findings share one root cause).

## P0
None. No data-corruption, money-path, or security-breach defect surfaced. The most damaging item (KYC write path) is **latent**, not currently broken on the running host â€” so it ranks P1, not P0.

## P1 â€” fix before they bite

**1. KYC + product-photo writability fix exists only on the live host, not in source â€” any volume/host recreate silently reintroduces BUG-2 EACCES (incl. legally-required KYC capture)**
- Where: `infrastructure/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml:88-90,116-118` + `apps/api-cloud/Dockerfile:30-43` + `apps/worker/Dockerfile:22-34`; consumers `apps/api-cloud/src/lib/kyc-store.ts:162` and `apps/api-cloud/src/lib/photo-store.ts:108`.
- Root cause: the `/data/photos` and `/data/kyc` ownership fix was hand-applied on the running container (manual `chown`/`chmod`); no Dockerfile or compose file creates/chowns `/data`, so a fresh volume mounts `root:root 0755` and the `uid 1000` node process hits 500 EACCES.
- Fix: in both runtime Dockerfile stages, before `USER node`, add `RUN mkdir -p /data/photos /data/kyc && chown -R 1000:1000 /data` (use `0700` on `/data/kyc`, not the live `0777`); rebuild + redeploy so the image matches the live hand-patched state.
- Why highest-impact P1: this is the one item with legal blast radius (GwG/DSGVO Ausweis capture) and zero in-repo self-heal â€” it dies silently on the next DR restore.

**2. tauri-pos document capture hard-500s in prod because R2 is unconfigured â€” sibling local-disk route was never wired in**
- Where: `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/photo-upload-url.ts:96-103` (via `apps/api-cloud/src/lib/r2.ts:25` throw); live consumer `apps/tauri-pos/src/screens/secondary/Dokumente.tsx:386`.
- Root cause: prod `.env` has `R2_BUCKET=` (empty), so `getR2Client()` throws â†’ routes re-raise as 500 on every normal upload, while the working local-disk route `POST /api/photos/upload` was never adopted by Dokumente.
- Fix: repoint `Dokumente.tsx` at `POST /api/photos/upload` (the migration the mobile/Fotos paths already got), or have the presign route return a clear "use local upload" contract instead of 500; delete the dead `uploadBlobToR2` + unused R2 presign routes.
- Scope correction (narrows the raw finding): product photography is **not** affected â€” `Fotos.tsx` already uses the local route; the cited `tauri-pos/.../photo-upload.ts:87` is dead code. Only document capture is broken.

**3. "eBay" toggle writes vestigial `listed_on_ebay`, which no eBay behavior reads â€” toggling never lists/enrolls anything, but the dashboard then shows a false "Online" badge**
- Where: writer `apps/mobile/src/app/product/[id].tsx:1098` â†’ persister `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products.ts:364`; the eBay channel is driven by `ebay_state` (`apps/mobile/src/app/ebay.tsx:138-143`).
- Root cause: the channel keys entirely on `ebay_state` (set only by a real marketplace publish, `products-ebay-publish.ts:184`); the toggle flips a legacy boolean, so `ebay_state` stays NULL and the item is never enrolled.
- Fix: remove the standalone toggle; route eBay listing through the eBay channel (`ebayApi.transition`/`publishToEbay`) as the `/ebay` screen already does, or show a read-only `ebayState` indicator that deep-links to `/ebay`.

**4. "Im Laden" toggle (`listed_on_storefront`) silently controls only WhatsApp-bot visibility â€” its label is wrong, and web-published items are invisible to the bot**
- Where: writer `apps/mobile/src/app/product/[id].tsx:1070` â†’ `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products.ts:363`; sole behavioral consumer `apps/api-cloud/src/lib/whatsapp-bot-tools.ts:64-65`.
- Root cause: storefront catalog/cart migrated to `is_published_to_web` (migration `0029`, `storefront-cart.ts:191-194`), leaving the deprecated flag wired only to the bot's `searchInventory` â€” so an `is_published_to_web=TRUE` item with the flag OFF is buyable on the web yet the bot insists it is unavailable.
- Fix: gate `searchInventory` on `is_published_to_web = TRUE` (match catalog/cart), then repurpose or remove the "Im Laden" toggle so the label matches its real effect.

## P2 â€” correctness/trust drift, no functional outage

**5. Dashboard inventory list shows false channel state â€” "Online"/"Im Laden" badges read legacy flags, never the real `isPublishedToWeb` gate** *(merged: raw findings #3 + #6 â€” same root cause)*
- Where: consumer `apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:703-721`; the field is missing from `packages/api-client/src/domains/products.ts` (`ProductListRow`) and never projected by `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products-list.ts:254-256`.
- Root cause: `is_published_to_web` is absent from `ProductListRow`, so the daily-scan surface can only render legacy flags; live prod shows zero correlation (all 14 web-published rows have `listed_on_ebay=FALSE` â†’ no "Online" badge; all 5 `listed_on_ebay=TRUE` rows are not web-published â†’ "Online" fires on non-web items).
- Fix: add `isPublishedToWeb` to `ProductListRow` + select/return it in `products-list.ts`; drive "Online" off `isPublishedToWeb` and relabel the `listedOnEbay` badge "eBay" (Tag glyph, not Globe).

**6. SOLD-but-still-published products hard-404 on their public PDP â€” 11 live indexed URLs dead, no "verkauft" state**
- Where: `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/storefront-catalog.ts:520-522` (detail gate includes `status='AVAILABLE'`).
- Root cause: the detail handler keeps `AND p.status='AVAILABLE'`, so a previously-indexed/linked PDP returns a hard 404 the moment it sells (11 such rows live), and `StorefrontProduct` carries no `status` field to render a sold state.
- Fix: drop `AND status='AVAILABLE'` from the detail gate (keep `is_published_to_web=TRUE`), add `status`/`isAvailable` to the schema + projection to render a 200 "verkauft" page, or return 410 Gone so crawlers de-index cleanly.

**7. api-client `ProductListRow` under-declares 7 stamp/collector fields the route actually returns (typed-away Briefmarken data)**
- Where: `packages/api-client/src/domains/products.ts:53-86` vs `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/products-list.ts:243-254`.
- Root cause: the route emits `stampErhaltung`, `stampMinr`, `yearMintedFrom`, `yearMintedTo`, `originCountry`, `period`, `catalogReference` on every row but the type declares none â€” inverse of BUG-1 (server sends more than the client types), so the mobile list can't type-safely render Michel number/Erhaltung.
- Fix: add the 7 optional/nullable fields to `ProductListRow` (they already exist on `ProductDetailRow`) + a contract test asserting the field sets match.

**8. Category facet ignores `hidden_from_storefront` â€” `?category=<hidden-slug>` can surface products filed under an internal-only bucket (e.g. Ankauf)**
- Where: `apps/api-cloud/src/routes/storefront-catalog.ts:400-420` (the `WITH RECURSIVE cat_descendants` EXISTS block).
- Root cause: the list display join and `/categories` tree both gate `hidden_from_storefront = FALSE`, but the facet's descendant CTE does not â€” latent today only because no published+AVAILABLE product currently sits under a hidden category (guard absent, not satisfied).
- Fix: add `AND hidden_from_storefront = FALSE` to the CTE base case and the recursive `JOIN categories child`.

## Pattern
Seven of eight are one disease: **a publish/channel flag honored on one surface but read from a different (legacy) field on another** â€” the eBay toggle, the "Im Laden" toggle, both dashboard badge surfaces, the SOLD PDP, and the category facet are all "UI/consumer reads field A; truth lives in field B (`is_published_to_web` / `ebay_state`)." Item 1 is the BUG-2 twin (write path not durably permissioned in source). A single consolidation â€” make `is_published_to_web` + `ebay_state` the only fields any surface reads or writes for channel state, and delete `listed_on_storefront`/`listed_on_ebay` as operator-facing controls â€” would close five of these at once.

## Single highest-priority action
**Encode the `/data/photos` + `/data/kyc` ownership fix in `apps/api-cloud/Dockerfile`, `apps/worker/Dockerfile`, and `docker-compose.prod.yml`, then rebuild + redeploy** (P1 item 1). It is the only finding with legal exposure (GwG/DSGVO KYC capture) and zero in-repo self-heal: it is one volume-recreate or DR restore away from silently breaking, and nothing in the source tree would catch it.
